other_observers: Coen Dexter; I found the bird and Coen also study the bird for several minutes.
specific_location: Just southeast of Nucla on Ash Street and 5th Ave. Cuckoo was about 200 yard south of intersection on Ash Street.
description: Bird was medium size, smaller than a Black-billed Magpie but similar shape. The bird had a medium long, all back bill that was curved downward. The back was all medium brown from head to tail. Underneath the bird was off white from the chin to the base of the tail with the chin and throat less white. The long tail was gray with alternating narrow light gray bars or spots and then wider dark gray bars. The eyes, in good light, were reddish brown and surrounded by a noticable orange-buff eyering. The legs and feet were dark. The bird was preening so good view of the wings both upper and lower, showed consistent brown color, somewhat lighter on the underside. The feathers on the back and wings were edged buff to whitish.Behaviors: I first notice the bird sitting in some brush with its head sticking out. I looked at it with my binoculars and did not know for sure what it was. Then it pop up and flew across the road and landed in a Russian olive tree. As good luck would have it the tree was near the road the the bird did not leave the tree for the next 30 minutes. The bird preened and would eat an occasional berry. At this point we, Coen was with me, knew it was a cuckoo. All field marks pointed to a Black-billed Cuckoo. We needed to get photos so Coen walked back home 1/2 mile away to get the camera. The cuckoo was still in the same tree until Coen brought me the camera. As soon as I tried to take photos the cuckoo began moved to the other side of the tree and therefore, the photos are not that great. I did have about 1/2 hour to study the bird at close range while sitting along the road waiting for the camera. That gave me a great chance to study field marks and as soon as the bird flew on we discussed what we had seen. In a few minutes after returning home we put the photos on the computer and again talked about the photos and the bird.Call: None heardPlumage: First fall plumage
